creator: se@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Ed Maste from comment #4)

MFC commit d767bf361b3 on 2023-03-08 has brought the inode accounting of FAT12/16 file systems into 13-STABLE.

I did not want this change to go into 13.2, since it had been developed too late in 13.2's development cycle and had not seen much testing on other systems than mine.

One reason for the MFC to 13-STABLE was that it contains a fix for a potential kernel panic (when mounting a large FAT12 drive/stick/partition with more than 2730 clusters), and it should therefore be included in 13.3.

A merge to the 13.2 release sources is possible, but since the panic has not been reported (it was detected while developing the FAT inode accounting patch since certain FAT12 file system parameters caused my regression test script to panic my system), I'm not convinced that a merge to releng/13.2 is necessary.

If there was a report of the panic being triggered (it leads to a page fault in the kernel, which does not seem exploitable beyond a DoS), a merge to 13.2 should be reconsidered, ÌMHO.
